channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kerala’s First Spring Census Reveals Majority of Natural Springs Under Private Ownership
item.description: The first-ever Census of Springs conducted in Kerala has revealed that nearly two-thirds of the state’s natural springs are privately owned. According to the report released by the Union Ministry of J...

item.title: Police discover suspected drug lab in Sandton following intelligence-led operation
item.description: During an early Sunday morning operation in Sandton, Gauteng police uncovered what appears to be an illegal drug manufacturing site following intelligence that pointed them to a house on Rembrandt Ave...

item.title: Tottenham lose 1-0 to Sunderland in De Zerbi's debut, remain in relegation zone
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur suffered a 1-0 defeat to Sunderland in Roberto De Zerbi's first match as head coach, intensifying concerns over their Premier League survival. The game remained goalless for the firs...

item.title: No winners in April 12 PCSO lotto draws; major prizes still unclaimed
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) confirmed that no bettors won the jackpot prizes in the April 12, 2026 major lotto draws. According to the PCSO report, no one matched the winning numb...

item.title: Kenya’s foreign exchange reserves ease to USD 13.3 billion, still above import cover requirement
item.description: The Central Bank of Kenya (CBK) reported that the country’s foreign exchange reserves declined to USD 13.316 billion as of April 9, 2026, extending a five-week downward trend. Even with the drop, the ...

item.title: Edward Kamau runs from Nairobi to Moshi to raise funds for charity
item.description: Edward Kamau set off from Nairobi on March 16 for a six-day run to Moshi, Tanzania, covering a total of 372 kilometres including the Kilimanjaro Marathon. His aim was not to chase personal records but...

item.title: Affordable Bullet Ice Makers That Rival Nugget Ice
item.description: This HuffPost article by Griffin Wynne explores the growing popularity of bullet ice as a budget-friendly alternative to the trendy nugget ice often found in Sonic drinks. While the GE Opal nugget ice...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Conclude Islamabad Talks Without Reaching Agreement
item.description: Vice President JD Vance visited Islamabad with the aim of resolving key disputes between the U.S. and Iran, including Iran's nuclear program and control over the Strait of Hormuz, but left after 21 ho...

item.title: Yas Tanzania expands rural digital connectivity through national network investment
item.description: Yas Tanzania has reaffirmed its commitment to improving digital access for underserved and rural areas across the country. This announcement came during the launch of 758 communication towers by Presi...

item.title: Saints hold firm to beat Port Adelaide as Butters reported for umpire abuse in fiery clash
item.description: In a dramatic conclusion to the AFL’s Gather Round, St Kilda edged out Port Adelaide at Adelaide Oval with a gritty 14-point victory that could shape their season. The Saints were the stronger side fo...

item.title: Hungary votes in pivotal election that may end Orbán's 16-year tenure
item.description: Hungary is holding what is being described as one of Europe’s most consequential elections of the year. Citizens are casting their votes to potentially unseat Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, who has been...

item.title: NAF Conducts Airstrikes in Jilli Axis Targeting Insurgents Amid Reports of Market Casualties
item.description: The Nigerian Air Force (NAF) don confirm say dem fighter jets carry out air operations for Jilli axis, Borno State as part of ongoing effort to dey fight terrorists for North-East. This one come after...

item.title: Public Schools, Religious Accommodation, and the Case for Secular Neutrality
item.description: In his National Post opinion piece, Dimitri Soudas reflects on the principle of secularism in Canadian public schools, prompted by the York Region District School Board's refusal to accommodate Greek ...

item.title: Poll Shows Most Israelis Reject Cease-Fire Efforts with Iran and Lebanon Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: A recent poll reported by The Wall Street Journal indicates that a majority of Israeli citizens are opposed to cease-fires with Iran and Lebanon as military operations continue in both areas. The surv...

item.title: Punjab Passes Stricter Law for Sacrilege of Guru Granth Sahib
item.description: The Punjab Cabinet has recently approved a significant amendment to the 2008 law concerning sacrilege, proposing life imprisonment for offences involving the Guru Granth Sahib. This move follows a ser...

item.title: KHRC report documents violence and discrimination against sexual and gender minorities in Kenya
item.description: A Kenya Human Rights Commission report titled Lives on the line: Violence and discrimination against sexual and gender minorities in Kenya examines abuses recorded between October 2023 and September 2...

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Crew Returns Safely from Lunar Mission, Prepares for Future Flights
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully returned four astronauts—Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen—after a nine-day journey around the moon. The Orion sp...

item.title: Melbourne-born Belinda Trembath leads Whistler, North America’s leading ski resort
item.description: Belinda Trembath, originally from Melbourne, has risen from a ski instructor in the Victorian Alps to vice-president and chief operating officer at Whistler, North America’s premier ski resort. Her ca...

item.title: Geordin Hill-Lewis outlines DA strategy to expand support and aim for national leadership
item.description: Cape Town Mayor Geordin Hill-Lewis has been elected as the new leader of the Democratic Alliance (DA) at the party's Federal Congress in Midrand. Hill-Lewis unveiled a clear strategy aimed at transfor...

item.title: Expert-Recommended Knee Braces for Injury Recovery and Support
item.description: Knee braces can provide essential support, stability, and pain relief for individuals recovering from injuries, dealing with joint pain, or engaging in physical activity. CNN Underscored consulted ort...
